title: Class 2 deterministic ordering — C206 trigger bucket priority (CLASS_1 first)
description: Deterministic scenario for the doc 12 §14 Phase 1.5 deterministic ordering layer. With routing_metadata.class2_scan_ordering_mode='deterministic' AND routing_metadata.class2_input_mode='scanning' active, the manager runs apply_scan_ordering after candidate generation. The C206 bucket in policy_table.global_constraints.class2_scan_ordering_rules.by_trigger_id specifies priority [CLASS_1, CLASS_0, CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, SAFE_DEFERRAL] — so the CLASS_1 lighting candidate is announced FIRST under scanning. The scan_ordering_applied audit field records matched_bucket='C206', applied_overrides=[] (no context overrides matched), final_order matching the announced order. Scenario asset only — does not authorize actuation.

steps.description: Publish a C206-triggering context payload (button_event with unmapped event_code) drives the policy router into Class 2 with trigger_id='C206'. environmental_context has all risk markers false (smoke=false, gas=false, doorbell=false) so no context override fires.

Class2ClarificationManager.start_session(input_mode='scanning', scan_ordering_mode='deterministic') initialises the session. After candidate generation (4 candidates: C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE/CLASS_1, C3_EMERGENCY_HELP/CLASS_0, C2_CAREGIVER_HELP/CAREGIVER, C4_CANCEL_OR_WAIT/SAFE_DEFERRAL), apply_scan_ordering runs with rules=class2_scan_ordering_rules and matches the C206 bucket [CLASS_1, CLASS_0, CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, SAFE_DEFERRAL]. session.scan_ordering_audit is set.

Per-option utterances follow the post-ranking order: '1/4. <C1_LIGHTING prompt>', '2/4. <C3_EMERGENCY prompt>', '3/4. <C2_CAREGIVER prompt>', '4/4. <C4 prompt>'. Note: this is the same as the source order in this case for insufficient_context — the C206 bucket happens to align with the static default order. Other triggers (C204, C207, C208) would re-order differently.

User picks single_click on option 0 → terminal CLASS_1. (The terminal selection itself is not what's tested here — what's tested is the ORDER in which options were presented and the audit attribution.)

Publish the terminal clarification record. scan_ordering_applied carries: rule_source='policy_table.global_constraints.class2_scan_ordering_rules', matched_bucket='C206', applied_overrides=[], final_order=['C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE', 'C3_EMERGENCY_HELP', 'C2_CAREGIVER_HELP', 'C4_CANCEL_OR_WAIT'].
The integration verifier asserts scan_ordering_applied.matched_bucket='C206' and scan_ordering_applied.final_order matches the candidate_choices order in the announced record.

transition_outcomes.condition: C206 bucket places CLASS_1 first; user accepts the first announced option.
transition_outcomes.example_confirmation: single_click on option 0 (deterministically the CLASS_1 lighting candidate)

notes: comparison_conditions tags BOTH dimensions because deterministic ordering is meaningful only under scanning. The runner sets routing_metadata.class2_scan_ordering_mode='deterministic' for this trial; class2_input_mode='scanning' must also be active (typically set by the deployment policy or by a sibling trial's class2_scanning_input condition).

Deterministic ordering (doc 12 §14 Phase 1.5) is opt-in. This scenario asserts the C206 bucket from class2_scan_ordering_rules.by_trigger_id is what produced the order — paper-eval can later swap the bucket value in policy and observe the order change without re-coding scenarios.
scan_ordering_applied is recorded ONLY when ordering ran (i.e., when both input_mode='scanning' AND scan_ordering_mode='deterministic'). Other modes leave the field absent.

Same candidate set, same Validator gating — ordering is pure permutation per doc 02 §10 invariant. Audit reviewers can compare scan_ordering_applied.final_order against announced scan_history to confirm no candidate was added or removed.

If the C206 bucket value changes in policy, this scenario's assertion 'expected_scan_ordering_applied_final_order_first_target=CLASS_1' may need updating — the verifier surfaces that mismatch immediately.
